title: Modeled heat transfer across battery pack
context: Built a Python heat-transfer model of a 12-cell lithium-ion battery pack using finite-difference methods.
details: Discretized each cell into a 3D grid and coupled the cells through shared surface nodes. Used a forward-time central-space explicit scheme with a stability-bounded time step.
outcome: Model produced stable temperature fields under all 8 operating scenarios tested.

title: Simulated 8 operating scenarios
context: Ran the model under 8 combinations of discharge rate and ambient temperature.
details: Swept 1C, 2C, and 3C discharge rates against ambient temperatures of 25°C, 35°C, and 45°C. Logged per-cell peak temperature and temperature uniformity for each scenario.
outcome: Identified a thermal runaway risk above 45°C under sustained 3C discharge.

title: Identified thermal runaway risk
context: Found a thermal runaway risk in the original cooling geometry under sustained high-discharge conditions.
details: Per-cell peak temperature exceeded the safety threshold above 45°C ambient under 3C discharge for the baseline geometry. The result was reproducible across all 10 random seeds.
outcome: Risk finding triggered the cooling-geometry redesign in the next iteration.

title: Proposed revised cooling channel geometry
context: Designed a revised cooling channel layout that improves flow distribution across the pack.
details: Increased channel diameter near the pack's center cells and added a flow-distribution manifold. Re-ran all 8 scenarios under the new geometry.
outcome: Improved peak temperature uniformity by 18% across the pack under the worst-case scenario.

title: Chose mesh resolution by stability analysis
context: Selected the finite-difference mesh resolution based on the explicit-scheme stability condition.
details: Computed the maximum stable time step at each candidate mesh size. Picked the coarsest mesh that still resolved the inter-cell temperature gradient.
outcome: Reduced per-step runtime by 4x relative to a uniform fine mesh with no measurable accuracy loss.

title: Validated against published cell-level data
context: Compared the model's per-cell temperature curves to published single-cell experimental data.
details: Used two published datasets from the lab's prior work as the validation benchmark. The model's predictions fell within the published experimental scatter band for both datasets.
outcome: Model passed the validation benchmark on both reference datasets.

title: Co-authored technical report
context: Wrote the methodology, results, and design recommendation sections of the project report.
details: Structured the report to mirror the lab's prior publication template. Embedded plots generated from the simulation runs.
outcome: Report cited by 2 subsequent student projects in the same lab.

title: Built post-processing pipeline
context: Wrote a post-processing pass that extracts per-cell peak temperatures and uniformity metrics from each simulation run.
details: Used xarray to slice the per-cell temperature time series and computed the requested metrics. Output written to CSV for downstream plotting.
outcome: Reduced post-processing turnaround from one hour of manual spreadsheet work to a few seconds.

title: Collaborated with the lab team
context: Worked alongside two PhD students and one postdoc in the battery lab.
details: Took part in weekly lab meetings, shared the simulation tool with the postdoc, and incorporated the postdoc's safety-threshold data into the model's outputs.
outcome: Simulation tool adopted by the postdoc for a follow-up safety study.

title: Presented to the lab group
context: Presented the methodology, results, and the cooling-geometry recommendation to the battery lab group.
details: Used slides generated from the post-processing pipeline output. Took questions on the assumed material properties and on the boundary conditions at the pack surface.
outcome: Recommendation discussed as a candidate for the lab's next prototype iteration.

title: Ran parametric study on cooling channel diameter
context: Varied the cooling channel diameter from 4 mm to 10 mm in 1 mm increments and recorded the uniformity metric.
details: Identified 7 mm as the diameter that maximized uniformity under the worst-case scenario while staying within the lab's flow-rate budget.
outcome: Identified the optimal diameter to recommend to the design team.

title: Produced thermal contour plots
context: Built a small Matplotlib routine that renders per-cell temperature contours for any scenario in the parametric sweep.
details: Used a shared color scale across all scenarios for visual comparability. Exported PNGs at 300 DPI for the report.
outcome: Contour plots became the centerpiece figures in the report and the lab presentation.
